title: Lock Bar Check
desc: The tower's doors are thick, the corridors are narrow, and none of that matters if the hidden lock bars start sticking.

steps.text:
In Gothos Tower, doors are rarely only doors. They are warnings, delays, filters, and occasional insults built on hinges. Most have a second function hidden somewhere in the frame: an internal crossbar, a viewing slit, a drop latch that can be thrown from the far side if the conversation begins going badly. The lower east corridor has started complaining through the metal, which means the lock bars are due for attention before they fail at an inopportune moment and make somebody else's temper a structural concern.

The security keeper carries a leather roll of oil pins and bar hooks. "Take the crossbars if you want the heavier work," she says. "Or test the shutter bolts and viewing slits if you prefer the narrow parts. Either way, I would like the corridor to stop sounding suspicious of itself."

options.label: Oil the lower crossbars and clear their guide slots
Test the shutter bolts and free the viewing slits

steps.text:
The crossbars slide inside the wall thickness on iron shoes that would move beautifully if iron enjoyed ash, dust, and damp. It does not. You withdraw each bar in turn, scrape the packed grit from the guide slot, oil the runners, and work the weight back and forth until the drag disappears. When the keeper throws the first restored bar home, it lands with a clean, satisfying finality that makes the whole corridor seem more convinced of itself.

By the third door the work has become rhythmic: draw, clean, oil, test, close. The keeper leans her shoulder into the last bar and listens to the lock take. "Good," she says. "A proper door should end arguments before they become interesting."

options.label: Roll the oil pins away and leave the bars traveling smoothly

steps.text:
The viewing slits are narrow shutters built into the black stone, each one meant to open silently and close without leaving any visible invitation behind. Two of them have seized from fine ash in the hinge teeth, and one bolt no longer seats fully into its receiver. You free the hinges, brush the channels, and test the bolts until the shutters snap open and shut with quick, discreet efficiency instead of scraping like an accusation.

The keeper checks each slit from the corridor side, then from the hidden alcove beyond, making sure the sightline is clear and the closure leaves no visible seam. When the last bolt slides home properly, she gives the wall a brief approving tap. "There. Privacy restored. Distrust, too, if all goes well."

options.label: Close the service panel and leave the corridor secure
